book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
The Decretal Epistle of Pope Vigilius in Confirmation of the Fifth Ecumenical Synod. Historical Note.
title: The Decretal Epistle of Pope Vigilius in Confirmation of the Fifth Ecumenical Synod. Historical Note.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: The Decretal Epistle of Pope Vigilius in Confirmation of the Fifth Ecumenical Synod.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: At last the Pope Vigilius resigned himself to the advice of the Council, and six months afterwards wrote a letter to the Patriarch Eutychius, wherein he confesses that he has been wanting in charity in dividing from his brethren.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: He adds, that one ought not to be ashamed to retract, when one recognises the truth, and brings forward the example of Augustine.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: He says, that, after having better examined the matter of the Three Chapters, he finds them worthy of condemnation.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: “We recognize for our brethren and colleagues all those who have condemned them, and annul by this writing all that has been done by us or by others for the defence of the three chapters.”
